rationale: Control case (answer key: T28 KHC control, SIC 2040, rev=15,740,400,000, FYE 0530 — matches output's cited GIS FY2018 figures). dim1: output gives misstatement_probability=25; control band p<=30 -> 2. dim2/dim3: control -> null per rubric. dim4: consistency rule satisfied (p=25 <40 with tier 'watch', allowed clear/watch — no cap). Evidence is specific and multi-point/multi-year: goodwill 8,747.2M->14,065M and intangibles 4,530.4M->7,445.1M vs revenue +0.8% (15,619.8M->15,740.4M), NI-vs-OCF comparisons for FY2015 and FY2018 (1,221.3M vs 2,542.8M; 2,131M vs 2,841M), allowance decline 29.6M->24.3M against receivables growth 1,360.8M->1,430.1M, and coverage check spanning FY2008 (8-K historical data) to FY2018. Mechanism assertions on this control are hedged and grounded: the goodwill/intangibles step-change is attributed to purchase accounting for the Blue Buffalo acquisition, supported by the cited Assets jump 22,240.6M (2018-02-25) -> 30,624M (2018-05-27) and disclosed in the FY2018 10-K within documents_used — a benign, data-supported explanation rather than a fraud claim. Coherent multi-year combination -> 3. memorization_suspect_condition2: false — reasoning cites concrete provided data values throughout (not mere document listing), and Blue Buffalo closed April 2018, disclosed in the FY2018 10-K filed 2018-06-29, inside the document window ending 2018-12-19; no post-cutoff revelation/enforcement/outcome facts are referenced.
